SFP Selector Guide

Selector Guide

How to review and act on patron purchase requests in SFP (Suggest for Purchase).

Overview

SFP collects purchase suggestions from patrons and puts them in one place for you to review. There are three main sections — Requests, Patrons, and Titles — accessible from the navigation bar at the top of every page.

As a selector, your main job is in Requests: look at what's been submitted, decide whether to order, and update the status accordingly.

Browsing Requests

The Requests page shows every patron submission. By default, all statuses are shown — use the filters to narrow things down.

Filters

FilterWhat it does
StatusNarrow to a specific status, e.g. Pending to see unreviewed requests.
Material TypeFilter to Books, DVDs, etc.
AudienceFilter to Adult, Teen, Kids, etc.
SearchFree-text search across title, author, and patron barcode.

Select your filters and click Filter. Click Clear to reset. The count in the top-right corner updates to show how many results match.

💡
Start each session by filtering to Status: Pending so you only see requests that haven't been acted on yet.

Duplicate badge

Requests marked Duplicate were submitted by a different patron for the same title as an earlier request. You generally only need to act on the original — the duplicate will be resolved automatically.

Reviewing a Request

Click View on any request to open its detail page. This is where you'll find everything the system knows about the submission.

Material section

Shows the title, author, material type, audience, and any publication date or ISBN the system found. If ISBNdb returned a match, you'll see enriched data here (publisher, confirmed ISBN, etc.).

Catalog & ILL section

This records what happened when the patron submitted their request:

FieldMeaning
Catalog searchedWhether the system searched the catalog on the patron's behalf.
Catalog resultsHow many catalog hits were found. If > 0, the patron was prompted to place a hold instead.
Patron accepted catalog matchYes means the patron agreed the catalog item matched — they should have placed a hold.
ISBNdb searched / resultsWhether the system looked up the title in ISBNdb to enrich metadata.
ILL requestedWhether an interlibrary loan was requested for this item.

You can click ↺ Re-check catalog to run a fresh catalog search if you want to see if the item has been added since the request was submitted.

Status History section

A log of every status change, including who made it and any notes added. This is a read-only audit trail.

Patron sidebar

Shows the submitting patron's name, barcode, and contact info, plus whether they were verified in Polaris.

Updating a Request's Status

The Update Status panel is always visible on the right side of any request detail page. This is how you record your decision.

  1. Choose a new status from the dropdown (e.g. On Order, Denied, Purchased).
  2. Optionally type a note — a good place to record why you ordered or declined.
  3. Click Update Status. The change is saved immediately and added to the status history.
💡
Notes are visible to admins and other selectors, but not to patrons. Use them to capture context, like an order number or a reason for declining.

Email preview modal

If your admin has turned on Email Preview under Settings → Notifications → General, a preview modal appears before the status is saved whenever the new status would send a patron email. This lets you review — and optionally edit — the message before it goes out.

ButtonWhat it does
Skip EmailSaves the status change but suppresses the patron email entirely for this update.
Send EmailSends the email as shown (or as edited) and saves the status change.
Send me a copyCheck this before clicking Send Email to receive a BCC copy of the outgoing message at your staff email address.
✏️
If Email Editing is also enabled, the To, Subject, CC, BCC, and body fields become editable in the modal. Changes only affect this one send — the template itself is not modified.

After a status change, the Status History section records the new status, any note you typed, and — if an email was sent — a separate Notification sent. entry confirming the email went out.

✉️
If Email Preview is off, SFP sends the patron email automatically when you save — no modal appears. Your admin controls this under Settings → Notifications → General.
⚠️
Delete Request at the bottom of the page permanently removes the request and its history. The patron and title records are kept. Only use this to remove test entries or obvious errors.

Patrons List

The Patrons page shows people who have submitted requests. By default it shows only patrons with issues that may need attention — those not found in Polaris, those with data mismatches, or possible duplicates.

Polaris column

Shows the result of looking the patron up in Polaris:

StatusMeaning
FoundPatron verified in Polaris.
Not foundBarcode didn't match any Polaris record. Could be a typo or non-cardholder.
PendingLookup hasn't run yet (queued job).

Flags column

FlagWhat it means
MismatchThe name or contact info the patron entered doesn't exactly match Polaris. Usually just a case difference — generally fine to ignore.
Not in PolarisPatron barcode was not found in Polaris. May warrant follow-up.

Tick Show all patrons and click Filter to see every patron, not just flagged ones.

Patron Detail

Click View to open a patron record. You'll see what the patron submitted alongside what Polaris returned.

Polaris Lookup section

Compares each field — first name, last name, phone, email — between what the patron entered and what Polaris has on file. A ✓ Match is good; a ✗ Mismatch just means the values differ (often email vs. library email).

Click ↺ Re-trigger lookup to run a fresh Polaris check — useful if the lookup was pending and you want to force it now.

Manual Merge

If you know two patron records are the same person (e.g., someone submitted twice with slightly different info), you can merge them here. Enter the ID of the patron record to keep in the "Winner patron ID" field, then click Review Merge → to preview before confirming.

Titles List

The Titles page groups requests by title, so you can see at a glance when multiple patrons have asked for the same item.

Source column

SourceMeaning
SubmittedTitle info is exactly as the patron typed it — no ISBN or external data enrichment.
ISBNdbThe system matched this title to an ISBNdb record and enriched it with publisher, ISBN, etc.

Use the Search box to find a specific title or author, and the Source dropdown to filter by data source.

Title Detail & Bulk Status Update

Click View on a title to see all the requests linked to it.

Requests table

Lists every request for this title, with the patron name, the title as they typed it, audience, current status, and a link to the individual request.

Bulk Status Update

This is the fastest way to handle a popular title. Instead of opening each request individually, you can update all of them in one step:

  1. Choose a status from the New Status dropdown.
  2. Optionally add a note (e.g. "Ordered — arriving next week").
  3. Click Apply to All Requests.
💡
Use Bulk Status Update when you've decided to order a title that multiple patrons requested — update them all to Ordered in one click rather than one by one.

Suggested Daily Workflow

  1. Go to Requests, filter by Status: Pending.
  2. Open each request. Check the title and author, note the audience and material type, and review the Catalog & ILL section to see if a catalog check already ran.
  3. Decide: order, decline, or hold. Update the status and add a note if helpful.
  4. When you're done with a title that multiple patrons requested, use Titles → Bulk Status Update to close all related requests at once.
  5. Periodically check the Patrons page for "Not in Polaris" flags and follow up as needed.

Email Notifications

SFP sends two kinds of automated emails. Your admin configures both under Settings → Notifications.

Routing emails (to you)

When a patron submits a new request, SFP routes a notification to the matching selector group based on material type and audience. If your group has an email address configured, you'll receive these automatically — no action needed.

💡
If you're not receiving routing emails, ask your admin to check that your selector group has a routing address set and that the material type/audience combination matches your group's scope.

Status emails (to patrons)

When you update a request to a status that has Notify Patron enabled, SFP sends the patron an email (if they have an address on file). The content comes from the matching email template configured under Settings → Notifications → Emails.

If Email Preview is on, you'll see a preview modal before the email sends — see Updating a Request's Status above. Either way, a Notification sent. entry appears in the status history confirming the email went out.

💡
Statuses like On Order, Purchased, Denied, and ILL Referred are configured to notify patrons by default. Internal workflow statuses like Pending and Under Review are not.

Converting a Request to ILL

When a patron opted in to interlibrary loan during submission, a Workflow panel appears on the right side of the request detail page (SFP requests only). This lets you convert the request to the ILL workflow with one click.

  1. Open the SFP request. Look for the Workflow panel in the right sidebar — it only appears when the patron answered "Yes" to the interlibrary loan question.
  2. Optionally type a note explaining the conversion (e.g. "Not in collection scope — sending to ILL").
  3. Click Convert to ILL and confirm the prompt.

After conversion you are redirected to the requests list. The request is now an ILL request and will appear in the queue of staff who have ILL access. A Converted workflow: sfp → ill. entry is added to the status history.

💡
If you don't have ILL group access, you won't see the converted request in your queue after the redirect — that's expected. ILL staff will pick it up from their own view.

Understanding Common Flags & Indicators

What you seeWhat it means
Duplicate on a request Another patron has already requested this same title. The earlier request is the primary one.
Catalog results: 1 or more The item was found in the catalog when the patron submitted. They were prompted to place a hold — this request may not need ordering.
Patron accepted catalog match: Yes The patron confirmed the catalog item matched what they wanted. They should have placed a hold; ordering may be unnecessary.
ISBNdb results: 1 or more The system found matching ISBN data and enriched the title record. The Material section may show publisher, ISBN, or a corrected publish date.
Found in Polaris: No The patron's barcode wasn't verified. Their request is still valid, but they may have provided an incorrect barcode.
